body.title: Coding domain's full Ollama ladder: Hex-local + Ollama-Cloud subscription, no other providers
body.text:
# D-coding-domain-hex-cloud-ladder-2026-07-01
**title:** Coding domain's full Ollama ladder: Hex-local + Ollama-Cloud subscription, no other providers
**date:** 2026-07-01
**status:** open
**spawned_tickets:** T-inference-coding-ladder-hex-cloud, T-consequence-D-coding-domain-hex-cloud-ladder

## Decision narrative
During rung-3 planning for the live DS/Hex proof, CC misread the registry domain-blind (mixed the coding-domain model list with the generalist list) and wrongly proposed Claude/Gemini as the coding escalation target and 'design=Claude-only' as a rule to supersede. Akien corrected: the coding domain's ladder is entirely Qwen (code) -> DeepSeek (design/architect), ALL on Ollama. Hex-local models are the free floor; Ollama-Cloud SUBSCRIPTION models (qwen3-coder:480b-cloud, deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud) are the escalation target ONLY because those flagships are too large to run on Hex's 32GB RAM -- 'coding tasks fail over on models that would be too large to run here.' Classification is out of this ladder entirely -- that work is moving to compiled graph-trees, not an LLM tier. No OR/Anthropic/Google belongs on the coding domain's ladder.

## Hypothesis
A coding-domain request that bumps to design-difficulty (after a capability failure on the code tier) routes to the local deepseek-r1:32b on Hex first; only when Hex's local candidates are unavailable does it escalate to deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud on the Ollama Cloud subscription -- never to OR/Anthropic/Google, at any difficulty rung, for this domain.

## Measurement Signal
route(task_class='worker', domain='coding', required_difficulty='design') returns source='ollama' model='deepseek-r1:32b' when Hex is available; with 'ollama' marked unavailable, it returns source='ollama_cloud' model='deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud'. Both cost_records show dollars=0.0. A live Hex dispatch at design-difficulty (rung-3 test) shows the DickSimnel log routing to deepseek-r1:32b/ollama.

## Intention
I intend that a coding-domain request escalates through the SAME family per rising difficulty rung, Hex-local first and Ollama-Cloud subscription only when a task genuinely needs a model too large for Hex to hold -- so cost stays $0 until that point, and no other provider is ever in the loop for this domain.
